title: Nav chrome still dominates excerpts across multiple exchanges
query: Binance spot trading rate limits, Bitget copy trading API, Coinbase Advanced Trade create order, Kraken authentication, Deribit options API authentication, Upbit order book API
observed: Nav chrome excerpts (Skip to main content, sidebar nav) persist. Binance: 3/10, Bitget: 3/3, Coinbase: 2/3, Kraken: 1/3, Deribit: 1/3, Upbit: 1/3, Coinone: 2/5, Bithumb: 4/5. All have excerpt_start near 0.
expected: _is_nav_region() should filter nav chrome before excerpt extraction
evidence: Answer correctness verification: 10/32 top-3 citations failed due to nav chrome. Worst: Bitget (100% nav), Bithumb (80% nav).

title: Multi-exchange query still silently drops second exchange
query: How do Binance and OKX authenticate?
observed: Returns 10 Binance claims and 0 OKX claims. No conflict status, no clarification field.
expected: Should return status=conflict, clarification field, or results from both exchanges
evidence: Identical to run #1 finding. OKX completely absent from results.

title: Embedding model cold start still causes 35-40s delays
observed: First semantic search in each process invocation loads Jina v5-text-small MLX model in 35-40s. Questions routing through semantic search take 40-180s total. Kraken futures overview: 84s, Bitget error code: 177s, Binance futures: 97s.
expected: Model should be pre-loaded or cached across invocations
evidence: Improved from run #1 (70-94s) but still makes interactive use impractical for semantic-heavy queries

title: Bare error codes still return unknown
observed: Both -1002 and 50111 return status=unknown with note 'Could not determine exchange'. search-error command finds matches for both.
expected: Answer pipeline should use search-error results or the error_codes signal from classifier (which includes exchange_hint)
evidence: Classifier correctly identifies -1002 as error_message with exchange_hint=binance but answer pipeline ignores this

title: Code snippets with numeric literals still misclassified as error_message
observed: Classified as error_message (conf=0.40, code=30000) instead of code_snippet. Also: client.create_order('ETH/USDT', 'market', 'sell', 50000) → error_message (conf=0.80, code=50000 mapped to OKX)
expected: Function call pattern (method_name + parentheses + string args) should override generic numeric error detection
evidence: \d{5,6} error pattern captures prices/quantities. 50000 even gets high confidence (0.80) because it matches OKX error pattern.

title: Excerpt byte-offset drift persists across Docusaurus-hosted exchanges
observed: 10/32 verified citations have excerpt_start:excerpt_end that don't exactly match the excerpt text (mixed_pass). Likely zero-width spaces or Unicode normalization differences.
expected: Exact byte-offset match between cited excerpt and stored markdown
evidence: Answer correctness check: 12 clean_pass, 10 mixed_pass, 10 fail

title: Bybit cross-section pollution still severe
observed: 2/9 claims are relevant (v5/ws, v5/websocket). 7/9 are from P2P, Pay, skills, ads sections. pay-docs/skills/SKILL and p2p/ad URLs are particularly egregious.
expected: Section routing should restrict results to websocket/v5 sections when query mentions WebSocket
evidence: c2: p2p/guide, c4: pay-docs/scan-payment, c6: pay-docs/skills/SKILL, c7: p2p/ad/update-list-ad, c8: pay-docs/recurring-payments, c9: p2p/ad/post-new-ad

title: crypto_com internal ID not recognized by answer pipeline
observed: 'crypto_com rate limits' returns status=unknown. 'Crypto.com rate limits' returns status=ok.
expected: Both user-facing name and internal ID should be recognized
evidence: Endpoint DB uses crypto_com, answer pipeline only matches Crypto.com

title: 14 pages with 0 word count still present
observed: 14 pages with word_count=0. Gemini: 9 pages (root, intro, prediction-markets, rest, websocket fast-api pages). Plus ccxt root, mercadobitcoin spec page, paradex.
expected: Pages should either have content or not be in the store
evidence: SELECT count(*) FROM pages WHERE word_count=0 returns 14

title: 400 Bad Request misclassified as question instead of error_message
observed: Classified as question (conf=0.20) instead of error_message. HTTP status code + message pattern not recognized.
expected: HTTP error patterns (4xx/5xx + status text) should be classified as error_message
evidence: classify_input('400 Bad Request') → input_type='question', confidence=0.20, signals={}

title: Binance authentication answer has hallucinated citations
query: How do I authenticate to Binance?
observed: Claims c2 and c3 cite Binance derivatives pages but the excerpt text does not appear in the stored markdown at the given byte offsets. Grade: fail (excerpt not found in page content).
expected: All cited excerpts should be verifiable in source pages
evidence: 2/3 top claims failed source verification for Binance auth query

title: Short queries without exchange name all return unknown
observed: All 5 single-word or short queries without an exchange name return status=unknown. This is consistent behavior but limits discoverability.
expected: Could potentially return multi-exchange results or suggest adding an exchange name

title: All 20 adversarial inputs handled gracefully
query: SQL injection, FTS5 injection, empty, unicode, path traversal, XSS, extreme length
observed: Every hostile input returned valid response with status=unknown, rc=0. No crashes, hangs, or information leakage. Extreme length (50K chars) completed in 0.1s.

title: Golden QA: 100% status accuracy, 88% URL hit rate
observed: 25/25 status match. 22/25 URL match. 25/25 classification match. 3 misses: Binance API key permissions, Coinbase exchange auth, bitstamp payload.

title: Non-semantic query paths are fast (sub-second)
observed: Endpoint lookup: 0.0-0.2s. FTS-routed questions: 0.3-1.4s. Semantic-routed questions (warm): 0.7-2.8s. Classifier: <0.05s. All adversarial: <0.1s.

title: FIXED: HTX queries no longer return changelog entries
observed: Run #1 found all 4 claims were changelog entries. Run #2: 0/4 claims are changelog entries. All 4 cite actual documentation pages (huobiapi.github.io).
